name: Bioprinting Resolution
description: Minimum feature size achievable in bioprinting (3D printing of living cells and biomaterials). Smaller values indicate higher resolution and finer structural control. Tracks progress from extrusion-based to light-based and acoustic bioprinting.

data_points.source: Boland et al. — early inkjet bioprinting of cells achieving ~500um minimum feature size
Guillotin & Guillemot — laser-assisted bioprinting (LAB) achieving ~300um resolution with cell viability
Organovo — NovoGen bioprinting platform for liver tissue; extrusion-based with ~200um features
Kang et al. (Wake Forest) — ITOP system printing human-scale tissue constructs with ~100um resolution
Grigoryan et al. (Rice/UW) — SLATE stereolithography bioprinting with ~50um features for vascular networks
Bernal et al. — volumetric bioprinting achieving ~25um feature resolution
Two-photon polymerization bioprinting — sub-cellular resolution approaching 5-10um demonstrated in research settings
